channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Modified Tejas Mk1 Twin-Seater to Serve as CATS MAX “Mothership” with ACID AI Combat System
item.description: India’s Combat Air Teaming System is entering a more advanced phase, with a modified Tejas Mk1 twin-seat variant being prepared to function as the dedicated “mothership” platform for CATS MAX, according to sources who have informed idrw.org. The development marks a critical step in operationalizing manned-unmanned teaming within the Indian Air Force’s future air combat doctrine.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL IMRH to Feature Five-Bladed Articulated Rotor, Moving Away from Dhruv’s Hingeless Design
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited is set to adopt a five-bladed articulated rotor system for its upcoming Indian Multi-Role Helicopter (IMRH), marking a significant departure from the hingeless rotor architecture used on the Advanced Light Helicopter Dhruv and the Light Combat Helicopter. The shift reflects the fundamentally different performance and load requirements of a 13-tonne class medium-lift platform compared to smaller utility and attack helicopters.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO’s Stratospheric Airship Set to Revolutionize IAF Surveillance as a “Mini AWACS” Force Multiplier
item.description: India is steadily advancing toward a new paradigm in airborne surveillance, with the Defence Research and Development Organisation’s (DRDO) High Altitude Platform System (HAPS) emerging as a potential game-changer for the Indian Air Force (IAF). Designed as a stratospheric airship operating at extreme altitudes, this system is increasingly being viewed as a “Mini AWACS” that could complement—and in specific scenarios even outperform—traditional jet-powered Airborne Warning and Control Systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Sheshnaag-150 Swarming Drone Successfully Tested at Pokhran, Demonstrates 720 Km Strike Range
item.description: India has taken a significant step toward next-generation autonomous warfare with the successful testing of the Sheshnaag-150 swarming attack drone at the Pokhran test range. Developed by Bengaluru-based startup NewSpace Research and Technologies (NRT), the system represents a decisive move into long-range, low-cost loitering munition capabilities designed to operate in highly contested environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Pushes for Astra-2 and BrahMos-NG another dozen Indian weapons Integration in 114 Rafale Fighter Deal With Dassault
item.description: India’s negotiations with Dassault Aviation for the acquisition of 114 Rafale fighter jets are entering a critical phase, with the focus shifting beyond procurement to long-term sovereignty over upgrades and weapons integration. At the heart of these discussions lies the Interface Control Document (ICD)—a crucial technical framework that will determine India’s ability to integrate indigenous weapons and upgrade the aircraft independently in the future.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Moves Ahead With $3.5 Billion P-8I Deal Despite Sharp Price Surge
item.description: Despite a steep and “prohibitive” rise in costs, India is moving forward with the acquisition of six additional P-8I Poseidon maritime reconnaissance aircraft from the United States under the Foreign Military Sales (FMS) route. The deal, which has been under negotiation for over a year, reflects the Indian Navy’s continued reliance on the platform’s unmatched operational capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Stryker Deal Collapses as Indian Army Backs Amphibious, Indigenous Wheeled ICVs
item.description: India has effectively stepped away from the proposed procurement of the Stryker armoured vehicle from the United States, with sources indicating that the program is now “off” as the Indian Army has shown limited operational interest in the platform. The development marks a significant shift toward prioritizing indigenous solutions in the wheeled infantry combat vehicle (ICV) segment.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Russian LNG under US sanctions heads for the first time to India
item.description: A cargo of liquefied natural gas from Russia’s Portovaya plant on the Baltic Sea that is under US sanctions is on its way to India, LSEG shipping data showed on Wednesday.If it reaches its destination, it would be the first such delivery to India since US President Donald Trump last year said Prime Minister Narendra Modi had assured him India would stop buying Russian energy.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pak orchestrated drug smuggling into J-K to fund terrorism for decades: LG Sinha
item.description: Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha on Wednesday blamed Pakistan for orchestrating drug smuggling into J-K to fund terrorism and destroy the young generation. The LG vowed to map every hotspot and said that each one of them will be hit like a surgical strike and the entire network will be dismantled.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Flags Risks From Conflict, Energy Shocks
item.description: India faces rising risks from global conflict and energy price volatility, but enters the new financial year with a strong macroeconomic position, Chief Economic Advisor V. Anantha Nageswaran said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tembo Global Industries’ arm gets licence for manufacture of ammunition
item.description: Tembo Global Industries’ subsidiary — Tembo Classic Engineering (TCEPL), has been granted licence by the Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India, for the manufacture of ammunition of various calibers.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Future Long Range Assault Aircraft Officially Named MV-75 Cheyenne II
item.description: Bell Textron Inc., a Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT) company, announced today that the U.S. Army officially designated the MV-75 as the Cheyenne II. Consistent with the U.S. Army tradition of honoring Native American tribes, the MV-75 honors the heritage of the Cheyenne Tribes, two federally recognized nations: the Northern Cheyenne Tribe in Montana and the Cheyenne & Arapaho Tribes in Oklahoma.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: US envoy launches ‘Freedom250’ in New Delhi, highlights US-India ties
item.description: In a colourful and symbolic start to America’s milestone anniversary, US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor has begun the ‘Freedom250 celebrations’ from New Delhi, unveiling vibrant auto-rickshaws (autos) adorned with images of US President Donald Trump and iconic American symbols.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Five Killed In Explosion At A House In Andhra Pradesh
item.description: Five people were killed and 20 others injured in an explosion at a house in Andhra Pradesh’s Sri Sathya Sai district on Wednesday, police said. The incident occurred in Kummaravandla Palli village of Kadiri mandal.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Fierce Encounter Between Security Forces And Maoists In Jharkhand’s Chaibasa
item.description: A fierce encounter took place on Wednesday between security forces and a banned Naxalite (Maoist) outfit in the Saranda forest region of Jharkhand’s Chaibasa district, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin
